channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court Orders Olabisi Onabanjo University Hospital to Reinstate Nurse and Pay Back Pay
item.description: The National Industrial Court of Nigeria for Lagos don give judgement wey favour Mrs. Ajibola Aishat Oluwafunke, nurse wey dem sack from Olabisi Onabanjo University Teaching Hospital in November 2020....

item.title: Rescue operations continue on Mount Beerwah following multiple hiking accidents
item.description: Emergency services returned to Mount Beerwah in Queensland to rescue an injured hiker several days after a tragic incident claimed the life of an 18-year-old woman. The woman and her partner had previ...

item.title: Teboho Mokoena’s brace lifts Mamelodi Sundowns to top of Premiership with eighth consecutive win
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns reclaimed the summit of the Betway Premiership standings with a 3–1 triumph over Marumo Gallants at Loftus Versfeld Stadium on Tuesday. The victory extended their winning streak to e...

item.title: Canada’s Electricity Generation Expected to Double by 2050, Wind Energy Leading Growth
item.description: The Canada Energy Regulator (CER) has released a report forecasting substantial growth in the country’s electricity generation by 2050, largely driven by rising demand from emerging technologies like ...

item.title: Understanding the drug crisis in Chatsworth and Phoenix: Causes and Community Responses
item.description: Recent reports have highlighted the persistent drug crisis affecting Chatsworth and Phoenix, showing that addiction often begins not with hardened criminals, but with vulnerable young people seeking b...

item.title: Understanding the Persistence of Ancient Astronaut Theories
item.description: The idea that extraterrestrials assisted in the construction of ancient monuments, popularised by Swiss author Erich von Däniken in his 1968 bestseller 'Chariots of the Gods', continues to captivate a...

item.title: Over 500 Ofoni Community Members Join APC, Pledge Support to Bayelsa Government
item.description: Over 500 members of the Ofoni Federated Communities in Sagbama Local Government Area, hometown of the late Deputy Governor of Bayelsa State, Senator Lawrence Ewhrudjakpo, have formally left the People...

item.title: BISP clarifies its role after tragic roof collapse during cash distribution in Rahim Yar Khan
item.description: On March 16, 2026, a tragic incident occurred at a retail shop in Rahim Yar Khan's Tibba area where at least seven women lost their lives and more than 75 were injured when the roof collapsed during t...

item.title: Tribunal suspends Colonel Purohit’s retirement pending decision on promotion and service benefits
item.description: The Armed Forces Tribunal (AFT) has temporarily stayed the retirement of Colonel Shrikant Prasad Purohit, who was acquitted in the 2008 Malegaon blast case. Colonel Purohit had sought promotion to the...

item.title: Challenges and Limitations of the University Affiliation System in India
item.description: The university affiliation system in India, long regarded as the backbone of higher education, is increasingly facing criticism for its inefficiencies and rigidities. Under the current model, universi...

item.title: Trump Criticises NATO After Allies Decline to Escort Ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump has strongly criticised NATO and several key allies for refusing to join Washington’s efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amidst an escalating conflict with Iran. Speaking...

item.title: The Long-Term Financial Impact of a UK Energy Bailout
item.description: UK Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer has pledged to support households facing rising energy bills as a result of the ongoing conflict in Iran. The article highlights the financial challenges associated w...

item.title: Charlotte Love Creates Playful and Expressive Food Illustrations
item.description: Charlotte Love, an imaginative artist, brings ordinary food to life with humor and charm through her whimsical illustrations. By adding simple cartoon eyes and mouths to fruits, vegetables, and other ...

item.title: Ruto and Gachagua Exchange Personal Insults Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The political rivalry between President William Ruto and his former deputy, Rigathi Gachagua, is escalating into personal attacks as the 2027 Kenyan presidential race approaches. What initially was a ...

item.title: Atiku Urges Tinubu Govt to Prioritize Security Amid Rising Attacks in Borno
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar don express serious wahala concerning the recent bomb wahala wey happen for Borno State. For one post for 𝕏, Atiku talk say e dey pain am as the region don start ...

item.title: U.S. Counterterrorism Director Joe Kent Resigns, Citing Opposition to Trump’s Iran War
item.description: Joe Kent, the director of the U.S. National Counterterrorism Center, has resigned from his position, citing his inability to support the ongoing war in Iran. Appointed by former President Trump and co...

item.title: Majaliwa Remembers Magufuli’s Dedication to Tanzania and Good Leadership Values
item.description: Chato, Tanzania – On March 17, 2026, former Prime Minister Kassim Majaliwa took part in the fifth anniversary of the passing of former President John Pombe Magufuli. Majaliwa spoke highly of Magufuli,...

item.title: ADC releases interim manifesto highlighting security, tax reform, and social welfare priorities
item.description: On Tuesday, the African Democratic Congress (ADC) present im interim manifesto for Abuja, highlighting security reform, tax restructuring, and social welfare as top priorities. The party talk say the ...

item.title: Denver authorities remove PolkaDot chocolate bars containing illegal psychedelics
item.description: In Denver, Colorado, authorities discovered PolkaDot-branded chocolate bars containing psychoactive substances, including psilocybin and synthetic tryptamines, on store shelves in January 2026. Market...

item.title: Reflecting on the End of Bluesfest and Its Cultural Legacy
item.description: The Byron Bay Bluesfest, a cornerstone of Australian live music, was cancelled just 20 days before its 2026 start, leaving long-time attendees devastated. David Free, a seasoned Bluesfest-goer, recoun...
